page: Amazon Web Services c8i.large is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at 3.9 GHz. The instance provides 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. Cache specifications include 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 480 MB of L3 cache. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a significant divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance achieves top-tier single-core results, as demonstrated by a Geekbench single-core score of 2175.0 and strong single-threaded performance in gzip compression (34.3 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (14.34 GB/sec). However, multi-core and memory-bound metrics are notably weaker. The Geekbench multi-core score of 2747.0 and stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 2158.29 fall into the lower percentiles. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read-only bandwidth registering at 22.25 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 17.89 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8i.large offers a low-cost entry point into the Gen8 Intel Xeon family, providing modern instruction sets like AMX and AVX-512 without the overhead of larger instance sizes. However, the low memory capacity of 4.0 GB and the 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth limit its resource density. This instance is suited for single-threaded compute tasks, basic text processing, lightweight web serving, and small-scale development or compilation workloads that do not require high memory throughput or parallel multi-core execution.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.large is a compute-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It features 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core, 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory, and no local storage or GPU. The processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2175.0 and high single-threaded compression speeds. Conversely, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 2747.0 and uncached read bandwidth of 22.25 GB/sec. This instance provides a cost-effective entry point for single-threaded compute workloads, text processing, and lightweight utility services, though it is constrained by its low memory capacity and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It delivers strong single-core performance but exhibits limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.large is an Intel-based compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance delivering strong single-threaded performance and modern Intel instruction sets for lightweight, CPU-focused workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Support for Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets
Strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling
No local storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for single-threaded tasks and lightweight text processing
